meta.name: Cinnamon Rolls (Yeasted Sweet Dough Baseline)
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog
meta.description: A canonical yeasted sweet dough cinnamon roll baseline with reliable dough, filling, and bake logic. Includes guidance for either commercial flour or fine fresh-milled flour (choose one). Icing is intentionally left as optional placeholders for later (icing logic module), but basic options are included as optional ingredients/steps.

ingredients.label: Instant yeast
ingredients.notes: About 2 1/4 tsp. Active dry can be used; bloom first.

ingredients.label: Unsalted butter, melted and cooled slightly (dough)
Commercial flour option (use this OR fresh-milled option): bread flour or all-purpose
ingredients.notes: About 4 1/4 cups AP equivalent. Bread flour gives chewier rolls; AP gives softer. Weighing is best.

ingredients.label: Fresh-milled flour option (use this OR commercial option): fine-milled, sifted
ingredients.notes: Fresh-milled absorbs more. Sift out coarse bran for softer rolls. You may need +15–60 ml additional milk during mixing depending on flour.

ingredients.label: Extra milk (for fresh-milled flour adjustment)
ingredients.notes: Add 15 ml at a time if dough feels dry/stiff; up to ~60 ml total.

ingredients.label: Heavy cream (optional, for ultra-soft rolls)
ingredients.notes: Optional: pour over rolls just before baking for softer, richer results.

ingredients.label: Optional icing (logic later)
ingredients.notes: Icing logic will be expanded later. A basic vanilla glaze option is included as optional ingredients/steps.

steps.title: Choose your flour option
steps.body: Use either the commercial flour option OR the fresh-milled flour option (not both). For fresh-milled flour, fine-mill and sift coarse bran for softer rolls; expect higher absorption.

steps.title: Warm milk and combine with yeast
steps.body: Warm milk to about 100–110°F (38–43°C) (warm, not hot). Whisk in sugar and yeast. If using active dry yeast, let stand 5–10 minutes until foamy.

steps.title: Mix dough
steps.body: Add melted butter and eggs to the milk mixture and whisk. Add flour and salt. Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then knead (mixer with dough hook 6–10 minutes or by hand 10–12 minutes) until smooth and elastic. Dough should be soft and slightly tacky, not dry.

steps.title: Adjust hydration for fresh-milled flour (if needed)
steps.body: If dough feels stiff or dry (common with fresh-milled), add extra milk 15 ml at a time (up to ~60 ml) while kneading until dough becomes soft and pliable. If dough is overly sticky, add 10–20 g flour.

steps.title: First rise
steps.body: Place dough in a lightly greased bowl, cover, and let rise until doubled, 60–90 minutes depending on room temperature. Target dough temp after kneading: ~75–80°F (24–27°C).

steps.title: Make filling
steps.body: Mix brown sugar, cinnamon, and pinch of salt (optional). Have soft butter ready. Prepare optional nuts/raisins if using.

steps.title: Roll out dough
steps.body: Punch down dough and roll into a rectangle about 45x30 cm (18x12 in). Keep thickness even.

steps.title: Apply filling
steps.body: Spread soft butter evenly over dough, leaving a 1–2 cm (1/2 in) border on one long edge for sealing. Sprinkle cinnamon-sugar evenly. Add nuts/raisins if using.

steps.title: Roll and seal
steps.body: Roll tightly from the long side into a log. Pinch seam to seal. If the dough resists, let it rest 5 minutes and continue.

steps.title: Cut rolls
steps.body: Cut into 12 equal rolls (about 3.5–4 cm / 1.5 in each). Use unflavored dental floss for clean cuts or a sharp serrated knife.

steps.title: Second rise (proof)
steps.body: Place rolls in a greased 9x13 dish with a little space between. Cover and proof until puffy and touching, 35–75 minutes depending on temperature.

steps.title: Make-ahead option (overnight)
steps.body: After shaping, cover dish tightly and refrigerate 8–16 hours. Next day, let stand at room temperature 45–90 minutes until puffy before baking.

steps.title: Bake prep
steps.body: Heat oven to 350°F (175°C). Optional: pour heavy cream over rolls just before baking for extra softness.

steps.title: Bake
steps.body: Bake 22–30 minutes until golden and set. For accuracy, internal temp in center rolls should be ~190°F (88°C). If tops brown too fast, tent with foil.

steps.title: Optional: basic vanilla glaze (icing logic baseline)
steps.body: Whisk powdered sugar with vanilla, pinch of salt, and enough milk to reach a pourable consistency. Drizzle over warm (not hot) rolls.

notes: Sweet dough logic: soft, enriched dough should be tacky and elastic. Dry dough yields dense rolls.
Fresh-milled flour: sift coarse bran for tenderness; expect higher absorption and slightly longer proof times. A short 5–10 minute rest after initial mix can help hydration.
Proofing logic: Under-proofed rolls tear and bake dense; properly proofed rolls look puffy and spring back slowly when gently pressed.
Icing logic is intentionally minimal here and will be expanded later (cream cheese frosting, cooked icing, glaze profiles, etc.).

storage.refrigerator: Airtight: up to 5 days (rewarm for best texture).
storage.freezer: Freeze baked rolls (un-iced) up to 2 months. Thaw and rewarm, then glaze.
